title: medicago falcata var. romanica
intro: Medicago falcata var. romanica, also known as Roman medick, is a perennial legume native to Europe, with a definition that categorizes it as a type of wild alfalfa or clover plant. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high levels of isoflavones, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Roman medick include the potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce the risk of heart disease, and alleviate menopausal symptoms in women. Roman medick can be prepared for herbal use by making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves, flowers, and roots.
